Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Davis J. Reilly, Esq. of Bledsoe Diestel Treppa, Tel. No. 415.981.5411, appearing for the Cross Defendant;
Thomas Purtell, Esq. of Law Offices of Thomas Purtell, Tel. No. 415.722.6291, appearing for the Cross-Complainant Christina Sutton.

Both sides stipulate that the matter may be heard by Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead.

Matter called, no argument held.

Ruling: There being no argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Cross-Defendant Jake Ng's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions as follows: The motion is granted in part as follows: Plaintiff shall produce her attorneys' billing records relating to this action (CGC-14-541228) that reflect any fees or costs that plaintiff seeks to recover as damages in this action. Plaintiff may redact from the billing records any portions that reflect confidential communications between plaintiff and her attorneys, including any information transmitted by plaintiff to her attorneys in confidence, and any legal opinions formed or advice given by the attorneys. Plaintiffs may also redact from the billing records that she produces any portions that relate to fees or costs that plaintiff does not seek to recover as damages in this action. Any redactions must be focused and selective; wholesale redactions would be inappropriate. There is substantial justification for the positions of both parties; discovery sanctions denied. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)
